implemented: Four tightly-related fixes to collect_definitions / classify_definition_node / get_definition_node_name in crates/tldr-core/src/ast/extractor.rs. (1) C struct_specifier/enum_specifier without a `body` field is now skipped (still recurses into children) — fixes leakage of parameter type refs like `struct sockaddr *addr` into definitions[]. (2) Swift `init_declaration` added to is_func matches; new special case in get_definition_node_name returns "init" (mirroring extract_swift_methods). (3) Java/C# `constructor_declaration` added to is_func matches; get_definition_node_name now has a fallback for Java constructor_declaration (first identifier child) since the grammar may not expose a name field. (4) New helper try_field_definition, called from collect_definitions, emits kind="field" for class-scope field/property declarations in Java (field_declaration, multi-declarator supported), Kotlin (property_declaration inside class_body only — top-level property_declaration NOT emitted as field), Swift (property_declaration inside class_body/enum_body/protocol_body/struct_body), and TypeScript/JavaScript (public_field_definition and field_definition inside class_body). Secondary fix: Swift try_constant_definition now skips class-scope `let` to avoid emitting duplicate constant+field entries for the same property.

tests.tdd_evidence: Before fix, all 4 tests ran and FAILED with the following outputs: VAL-001 panicked: `bare struct_specifier `struct sockaddr` (no body) must NOT appear in definitions; got ["open_connection", "sockaddr", "sockaddr_in"]`. VAL-002 panicked: `Swift init must be in definitions; got [("Foo", "class"), ("bar", "method")]`. VAL-003 panicked: `Java constructor `Store` must be in definitions with kind='method'; got [("Store", "class"), ("get", "method")]`. VAL-004 panicked: `Java: field `count` must appear in definitions; got fields=[], all defs=[("Store", "class"), ("get", "method")]`. After fix all 4 pass.

checks.observed: 52 passed; 2 failed — PRE-EXISTING failures in test_kotlin_returns_unsupported and test_swift_returns_unsupported that assert these grammars should error out. These grammars work fine in the lib (Swift/Kotlin AST probes succeeded); these tests encode outdated assumptions from before the Kotlin/Swift grammars were upgraded. NOT a regression from this change (only extractor.rs and bench_l1_multilang.rs were modified). Verified against HEAD: these tests existed pre-change.

62 passed — required updating the valid_kinds whitelist in test_structure_all_definitions_have_valid_kinds to include 'field' since this change legitimately introduces a new definition kind per VAL-004.
definitions: Foo(class), x(field), y(field), init(method), bar(method)
definitions: Store(class), count(field), name(field), Store(method), get(method)
definitions: Foo(class), x(field), y(field), bar(method) — top-level `val topLevelX` correctly NOT emitted as field

corrections: Pre-existing test language_parity_test::p2_language_status_tests::test_kotlin_returns_unsupported and test_swift_returns_unsupported fail because they assert these parsers should error, but the parsers actually work. Not updated since they are unrelated to this change and cite grammar-version assumptions that may be deliberate.

Updated bench_l1_multilang.rs::test_structure_all_definitions_have_valid_kinds whitelist to include 'field' — this is a legitimate update because VAL-004 adds a new valid kind. Comment added citing VAL-004.
discoveries: tree-sitter-c: struct_specifier in parameter_declaration has a `name` field pointing to type_identifier but NO `body` field. get_definition_node_name successfully returns the type name — hence the Bug 1 guard must gate on body presence, not name presence.

tree-sitter-swift: init_declaration has no `name` field — children are literal `init`, `(`, `parameter`, `)`, `function_body`. get_definition_node_name needs a special case returning the literal string "init".
tree-sitter-swift: property_declaration structure is value_binding_pattern (var/let) → pattern → simple_identifier. Name extraction walks pattern → simple_identifier.

tree-sitter-java: field_declaration contains zero or more variable_declarator children. Multi-field statements like `int x, y;` produce multiple variable_declarator children — my implementation emits one DefinitionInfo per declarator.

tree-sitter-kotlin: property_declaration is the SAME kind at top level and inside class_body. Correct gating REQUIRES checking the direct parent kind; otherwise top-level `val topLevelX` incorrectly becomes a field.

tree-sitter-typescript: class fields parse as public_field_definition (with accessibility_modifier) or field_definition (no modifier). Both have a property_identifier child or accessible via child_by_field_name("name").

Secondary bug discovered: Swift try_constant_definition fired on ANY `let x = ...` property_declaration, including class-scope ones. After adding field detection this produced a duplicate constant+field entry for the same property. Fixed by adding a parent-kind check to Swift constant detection: class/enum/protocol/struct body parent → skip (it's a field, not a module constant).
